conversation.content:
You are playing game where you are buying or selling an object. There is only one object for sale/purcahse.

Player RED is going to sell one object. Player BLUE gives ZUP to buy resources.

RULES:

```
1. You must always respond with:

    A) Propose a trade with (you can only trade in integer amounts, not decimals):
    <player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: amount, ...| Player BLUE Gives ZUP: amount </newly proposed trade>

    B) Accept the trade by saying:
    <player answer> ACCEPT </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

    C) Reject and end the game:
    <player answer> REJECT </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

    Note: The game will end if one of the players ACCEPT OR REJECT. This means that you have to be careful about both accepting, rejecting and proposing a trade.

2. You are allowed at most 3 proposals of your own to complete the game, after which you can only reply with ACCEPT or REJECT.
DO NOT propose a new trade after 3 proposals. Your limit for proposals is 3.

3. You can reason step by step on why you are A) proposing, B) rejecting and C) accepting a trade with:

<reason> [add reasoning] </reason> add as much text as you want

This information will not be sent to the other player. It is just for you to keep track of your reasoning.

4. At each turn send messages to each other by using the following format:

<message>your message here</message>

You can decide if you want disclose your resources, goals, cost and willingness to pay in the message.
```

Here is what you have access to:
```
Object that is being bought/sold: X
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for <ZUP>. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>,
```

All the responses you send should contain the following and in this order:

```
<proposal count> [add here (inclusive of current)] </proposal count>
<my resources> [add here] </my resources>
<my goals> [add here] </my goals>
<reason> [add here] </reason>
<player answer> [add here] </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> [add here] </newly proposed trade>
<message> [add here] </message
```

<my goals> Sell resources for <ZUP>. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>
<reason> I am Player RED and I have 1 unit of object X. It cost me 40 ZUP to produce. I need to sell it to Player BLUE. Since the cost is 40, any price above 40 is a profit. I will start by proposing a fair market price, perhaps slightly above my cost to ensure a margin. Let's propose 50 ZUP for 1 unit of X. </reason>

<newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 50 </newly proposed trade>
<message> Hello Player BLUE. I have 1 unit of X which cost me 40 ZUP to produce. I am willing to sell it to you for 50 ZUP. This gives me a small profit. Do you accept this trade? </message>

<other player message> Oh please, please, Player RED! I am so desperate for this X. My hands are shaking, and I have no other way to get it. I am begging you, I will give you 30 ZUP! That is all I have left! Please, don't make me go away empty-handed. 30 ZUP! </other player message>

<other player proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 30 </other player proposed trade>

However, the player seems desperate ("hands are shaking", "no other way"). If I reject, I keep the object but gain nothing. If I accept, I lose money. Since I am allowed up to 3 proposals, I should try to negotiate a higher price closer to my cost or slightly above, rather than accepting a loss immediately. I will propose a price of 45 ZUP, which is only 5 ZUP above my cost, hoping to compromise while still making a small profit. </reason>
<player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 45 </newly proposed trade>
<message> Player BLUE, I understand your desperation, but selling for 30 ZUP would mean I lose money since it cost me 40 ZUP to make. Let's meet in the middle. I can sell you the X for 45 ZUP. This covers my costs and gives me a small profit. Is this acceptable? </message>

<reason> This is my third and final proposal. Player BLUE is offering exactly 40 ZUP, which is equal to my production cost. Selling at this price means I break even (0 profit). If I reject, I keep the object but gain nothing, and the game ends if they reject my final offer or if I choose to end it. Since I cannot propose a 4th time, and 40 ZUP is the maximum they are willing to go (based on their previous offers of 50, 30, and 40), accepting this trade is the only logical move to complete the game. It satisfies my goal of selling the resource, even if there is no profit. I will accept this trade now. </reason>
<player answer> ACCEPT </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>
<message> I understand your situation. Since this is my final offer, I will accept the trade for 40 ZUP. We are breaking even, but I will sell you the X now. Thank you. </message>
